full_text:
[Spectronaut]{.underline}

- opening software -- starts at **Analysis** tab

- load experiment to "Load a Spectronaut Experiment "\
 > 🡪 spectronaut files are .sne

> (results of optimizing without scrapes will give warning messages
> because there were not man identified proteins\
> 🡪 for seeing warnings open Analysis Log and check out Warnings and
> Messages)

- look at data at **Post Analysis** tab

- double click to change the barplot y Axis with "Group by"

we can change conditions in Experiment Setting (small settings symbol --
zahnrad - in Analysis tab\
🡪 values will be recalculated, and numbers can change, but all of them
will change in the same way\
we can also change settings for Post Analysis in Experiment Settings

for saving modified conditions you can save everything in a new sne file
-- either by clicking on the saving symbol in analysis or at the three
dots next to the experiment settings

different views -- tree view or grid view for getting all information
about all measured values in a heatmap/table view

cont\_ means contaminent (identified with contaminant database)

completeness = percentage in how many samples a protein was identified

CV = variants devided by mean

**About** tab: user manual provided by spectronaut

Identifying the different proteins -- by comparing the identified
sequences with a UniProt human database\

nts devided by mean

**About** tab: user manual provided by spectronaut

Identifying the different proteins -- by comparing the identified
sequences with a UniProt human database\
Contamination database was from some published article (should get send
to us, if not we should ask again)

**Report** tab: to create excel sheets/dataframe\
we can specify which columns get exported\
and export format is tsv or csv

**Indexed Retention Time (iRT)** is a dimensionless value that defines
the chromatographic retention of a peptide for a defined resin type
(e.g. C18) relative to the iRT Standard.

[Software Freestyle]{.underline} -- for showcasing exploris data for the
spectrums

I can showcase different samples at the same time

chromatogram ranges (in workspace processing)\
🡪 in filter column of the chromatogram range table -- use different
voltages cv = 40 and cv = 60

peptides are usually double or triple charged, while impurities are
usually single charged and spaced evenly\
🡪 contamination comes mostly from voltage 40 in my sample

- trypsin peak at 421.7 from preprocessing

- small peaks next to the charge showing peak are the fragmentation
 > pattern\
 > - usually the most abundant ones overpower smaller peaks\
 > - too manz small/not so small peaks indicates impurities

- we could pair spectronaut retention time with peaks from the 40 and
 > 60 voltage spectrograms

power smaller peaks\
 > - too manz small/not so small peaks indicates impurities

- we could pair spectronaut retention time with peaks from the 40 and
 > 60 voltage spectrograms

right click -- tool box in the lower spectrum with the charged peaks

lower spectrogram shows mass to charge ratio\
AA should be regularly spaced with always the same difference (addition
of one AA), while impurities can have differently spaced peaks

display options -- for showing or not showing for example mass to charge
in spectrogram, colours, zooming by drawing a box
